sections.paragraph:
to throw, to cast, i.q. יָדַד No. I.; kindred is הָדָה. Eth. ወደየ፡ id. Imp. יְדוּ Jer 50:14.

Piel, i.q. Kal, to throw, as stones. Fut. וַיַּדּוּ for וַיְיַדּוּ Lam 3:53. Inf. יַדֹּות Zec 2:4.

Hiphil הֹודָה, fut. יֹודֶה, sometimes יְהֹודֶה Psa 28:7 45:18 Neh 11:17.

(1) to profess, to confess; perhaps properly, to show or point out with the hand extended; from the idea of the hand being cast forth, i.e. extended (see Kal; comp. שָׁלַח יָד and יָרָה, Kal and Hiphil, to cast, and thence to shew by the extended hand). Arab. ودى Conj. X.; Syr. Aph. id. Constr. followed by an acc. Pro 28:13 and followed by עַל (concerning), Psa 32:5.

(2) to give thanks, to praise, to celebrate, since thanksgiving and praise naturally follow the acknowledgment or confession of benefits received; followed by an acc. Gen 29:35 49:8 Psalm 7:18 30:13 and לְ of pers. Psa 75:2 1Ch 29:13 Ezr 3:11. הֹודָה שֵׁם יְיָ to praise the name of Jehovah, 1Ki 8:33 Ps. 54:8 לְשֵׁם יְיָ id., Psa 106:47 122:4 .

Hithpael הִתְוַדָּה (Vav being taken in the place of Yod) i.q. Hiphil.

(1) to confess, prop. to confess concerning one’s self, to shew one’s self as guilty. Eth. አስተቀደየ፡ to accuse, to criminate; properly, I believe, to object, cast against; (Germ. vorwerfen, from the idea of casting; ውዴት፡ an accusation, Borwurf), Dan 9:4 followed, by an acc. of the thing, Levit. 5:5 16:21 26:40 עַל of the thing, Neh 1:6 9:2 .

(2) to praise, to celebrate, followed by לְ 2Ch 30:2.

Derived nouns, [יָד], תֹּודָה, הֻיְּדֹות, and the proper names יְדוּתוּן, יְדָיָה, יְהוּדָה, and those which are secondarily derived from them, יְהוּד, יְהוּדִי, יְהוּדִית, הִתְיַהֵד [also perhaps דָּת, דֹּתַיִן].
